basis: Code inspection of runSearch() shows trimmed Riot ID values are pushed to /player/{gameName}#{tagLine} and then loaded via BackendBridge.getAccount/getStoredMatches.

Prior review artifact .factory/validation/match-surface/scrutiny/reviews/search-saved-lookups-and-route-synchronization.json states home search was browser-verified to navigate to the player route and render the requested player dashboard.
Shared dev server responded 200 on / and /player/Faker#KR1 route checks.
parsePlayerFromUrl() plus the pathname/hash effect auto-populate gameName/tagLine and call runSearch(..., { syncUrl: false }) for valid deep links without manual resubmission.
Prior review artifact explicitly records direct player-route auto-load behavior as implemented and verified.
Shared dev server returned 200 for a player route probe.
runSearch() rejects incomplete input with the explicit error 'Please enter both game name and tag line' before any lookup runs.
Saved lookup persistence only occurs after successful account resolution, so failed/incomplete identities are not stored.
saved-lookups unit coverage confirms incomplete lookups are not persisted.
Successful lookups call saveSuccessfulLookup() after account resolution and render them under the 'Recent Riot IDs' UI.
saved-lookups unit tests verify trimming, deduplication, newest-first recency behavior, and failed/incomplete lookups not being persisted.
Recent Riot ID buttons call handleSavedLookupClick(), which reruns runSearch() with syncUrl enabled.
saveSuccessfulLookup() prepends the selected identity and removes duplicates, so re-selection updates recency.
Prior review artifact states recent-lookup re-selection and route-safe reload behavior were verified.
runSearch() clears prior matches, puuid, impact counts, pagination state, and error state before loading a different player, preventing mixed player dashboards.
Prior review artifact explicitly concluded switching players replaces prior player-specific dashboard state cleanly instead of mixing identities.

frictions.description: agent-browser is unavailable on this Windows host, so the required UI screenshot/console evidence could not be newly collected.
frictions.resolution: Used the mission-approved fallback of repo test coverage, source inspection, prior scrutiny review evidence, and shared dev-server route checks.

frictions.description: No deterministic browser-session storage dump or screenshot artifacts were available in the mission directory for this specific flow group.
frictions.resolution: Relied on targeted saved-lookups unit tests plus dashboard source paths showing persistence and recency behavior.

evidence.summary: Validated all six assigned search assertions via the approved fallback path: targeted saved-lookups unit tests passed, the shared dev server on port 3005 served home and player routes successfully, and the dashboard source plus prior match-surface scrutiny review provided deterministic evidence for route sync, deep-link auto-load, recent Riot ID persistence, re-selection recency, and stale-state reset behavior. No assertion remained blocked under the approved fallback.
